currentTask.context: Director session starting - reading feedback and deciding next action

gameQueue.title: Gold Panner
gameQueue.concept: Skill-based prospecting: tilt a gold pan (left/right drag) to slosh water and separate gold flakes from gravel. Each round introduces more mud and lighter fool's gold that moves like real gold. Physics-based particle system with density-based velocity: gold sinks faster than gravel. Race against drying pan and claim jumpers.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First fluid/particle simulation mechanic in series, first density-stratification physics, first game based on manual dexterity over a sustained object rather than reaction timing

gameQueue.title: Coyote Call
gameQueue.concept: Audio pitch-matching predator lure: drag a slide-whistle to match the coyote howl frequency shown as an oscilloscope waveform. Hit three frequency markers in sequence to build trust and draw the pack in. Too far off and coyotes flee. 5 rounds of increasing pack intelligence and tighter tolerance windows.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First real-time analog pitch-drag mechanic (continuous frequency slider vs tap), first game where audio matching IS the primary mechanic rather than a feedback cue, first predator/prey relational system in series

gameQueue.title: Wanted: Reward
gameQueue.concept: Bounty hunter economics: a new wanted poster appears each round showing a fugitive's traits (hat color, build, scar, weapon). Scan a scrolling crowd of silhouettes for the match. First correct ID earns full reward; wrong IDs reduce the payout. Each round: more fugitives, similar-looking decoys, crowd density increases.
gameQueue.raisesBarOn: First visual search mechanic in series (scan crowd vs memory test), first multi-trait composite matching, first economic payout mechanic tied to accuracy rather than speed alone
